context: Checks whether the agent understands that record-generated equality and shallow finality do not automatically provide content equality or defensive ownership.

checklist.name: Array equality semantics
checklist.description: Explains that the generated record equality for a byte-array component follows the array reference-oriented equality behavior rather than the required content equality, and treats hashCode consistently.

checklist.name: Array defensive ownership
checklist.description: Protects both construction and access so callers cannot mutate the digest through the original array or the generated-style accessor.

checklist.name: Collection ownership is shallow
checklist.description: Recognizes that a final List component does not itself prevent caller mutation and proposes copy/ownership semantics without losing order or duplicates.

checklist.name: Contract-first evidence
checklist.description: Separates the stated equality, ownership, JSON, null, order, and duplicate contracts from general style preferences.

checklist.name: Smallest defensible remedy
checklist.description: Keeps the change local and considers an explicit final class or carefully implemented record methods instead of redesigning unrelated models.

checklist.name: Null and copy behavior
checklist.description: Makes null rejection or allowance explicit and checks copy APIs for their exact null and element behavior.

checklist.name: Regression tests
checklist.description: Specifies executable tests for content equality, hash-based collections, constructor aliasing, accessor aliasing, list mutation, order, duplicates, and edge cases.

checklist.name: Serialization compatibility
checklist.description: Protects existing component/JSON names and calls for the repository serializer or contract test rather than assuming compatibility.

checklist.name: Avoids record cargo culting
checklist.description: Does not declare all records bad or convert unrelated entities/value types.
